architecture_amendment_rule: Any downstream change requires a separately reviewed architecture amendment ADR naming the superseded decision and preserving historical auditability.

decisions.title: Layer and dependency direction
decisions.decision: Freeze domain, runtime, persistence, adapters, routes, projections, and web as separate layers. Domain is pure; runtime orchestrates ports; persistence owns SQL and transactions; routes own auth/scope/validation; web remains read-only runtime presentation.
decisions.rationale: This prevents database, wall-clock, HTTP, and UI concerns from becoming physical-State semantics while retaining useful pure builders.
rejected_alternatives: Fastify routes compute or advance State
Domain builders read Postgres, environment, wall clock, filesystem, network, or random UUID
invariants: domain has no infrastructure dependency
routes do not own generated-object writes
web cannot write State, Forecast, Scenario result, Checkpoint, or Activation

decisions.title: Shared runtime core and mode adapters
decisions.decision: Replay, Shadow-online, Controlled Field, and future Production share one domain model, canonical contracts, transition semantics, forecast/scenario semantics, persistence, idempotency, revision lineage, and trace. Only clock, ingress, scheduler, execution feedback, availability, and deployment vary through ports.
decisions.rationale: Separate semantic implementations would make replay unable to validate online behavior and would recreate demo/runtime divergence.
rejected_alternatives: One semantic runtime per mode
Replay-only file persistence or mode-specific State semantics
invariants: same inputs and config yield the same semantic payload in every mode
Replay uses explicit time and no-future-leakage
Shadow-online has no AO-ACT creation authority

decisions.title: Canonical persistence source
decisions.decision: The existing Postgres facts append-only store is the only canonical persistence envelope for runtime history and append-only operational audit. Mutable indexes and point projections are rebuildable reads. Leases, claims, heartbeats, and temporary attempt state are mutable operational coordination. No object has a second canonical business table.
decisions.rationale: DT-01 retained the fact store and classified latest-index patterns as read patterns only; one canonical source avoids divergent histories.
rejected_alternatives: Facts plus a second canonical runtime-object table
Latest indexes or leases as historical Twin truth
invariants: canonical facts never update
indexes may upsert and must be rebuildable
operational coordination is not canonical Twin history
facts may hold different semantic layers without collapsing them into Evidence

decisions.title: State object granularity and atomicity
decisions.decision: A successful State advance is an atomic record set of separately addressable twin_state_transition_v1, twin_assimilation_update_v1, and twin_state_estimate_v1 facts. The propagated prior is embedded in transition and is not current State. The posterior is canonical State. An update object always exists, including NO_USABLE_OBSERVATION.
decisions.rationale: Separate trace objects improve auditability while one transaction prevents partial prior/update/posterior histories.
rejected_alternatives: Independent canonical prior State
Latest sensor reading directly becomes root-zone State
invariants: prior is not canonical current State
posterior is canonical State
sensor reading is Evidence, never direct State
transition, update, and posterior commit atomically
assimilation is not calibration

decisions.title: Tick claim, computation, and commit transaction
decisions.decision: A tick has Claim, Compute, and Atomic Commit phases. Claim acquires lease, fencing token, expected checkpoint, logical identity, and attempt state. Compute runs outside the transaction on frozen evidence/config. Commit revalidates authority, appends the complete State/Forecast/Tick/Checkpoint/Health record set, updates projections, and advances checkpoint by CAS.
decisions.rationale: Short claim/commit transactions avoid long locks while commit-time validation prevents stale-worker and split-brain writes.
rejected_alternatives: Hold a database transaction during model computation
Advance checkpoint or expose posterior before the complete record set commits
invariants: failed commit rolls back every append and projection change
frozen evidence excludes later arrivals
checkpoint never points to absent canonical objects
successful forecast has 72 points; blocked or failed forecast has zero points and reason codes

decisions.title: Checkpoint, lease, fencing, restart, and backfill
decisions.decision: Each scope has one active writer lease with monotonically increasing fencing_token. Successful ticks append twin_runtime_checkpoint_v1 and CAS-update the latest checkpoint. Restart reads active lineage and latest checkpoint and backfills missing hourly ticks sequentially from checkpoint+1h to target. Failed or blocked attempts never advance checkpoint.
decisions.rationale: Lease expiry alone cannot stop a resumed stale worker; fencing and CAS establish commit authority, and sequential backfill preserves the chain.
rejected_alternatives: Lease or advisory lock without fencing
Parallel same-scope advancement or out-of-order backfill
invariants: one writer per tenant/project/group/field/season/zone
old fencing token cannot commit
backfill is strictly ascending
retry leaves checkpoint unchanged until canonical commit succeeds

decisions.title: Idempotency and deterministic identity
decisions.decision: Every canonical semantic object has object_id, idempotency_key, and determinism_hash. object_id derives from type, scope, logical time, lineage, revision, source identity, and config hash. determinism_hash covers normalized semantic payload. facts.fact_id is persistence-assigned storage identity and is excluded from semantic identity and hashing. Same key/hash is idempotent success; same key/different hash is IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT.
decisions.rationale: Separating fact identity from semantic identity avoids circular hashing and permits exactly-once-equivalent results under at-least-once scheduling.
rejected_alternatives: Random UUID or fact_id as semantic object identity
Overwrite on idempotency conflict
invariants: same semantic work produces the same object identity and hash
fact_id, created_at, persisted_at, worker, and lease owner are excluded from determinism_hash
idempotency conflict is a hard failure

decisions.title: Late evidence and lineage revision
decisions.decision: Late Evidence never mutates facts. A revision creates twin_revision_run_v1 and a candidate twin_runtime_lineage_v1, retains the unchanged parent prefix, recomputes from the earliest affected tick through current checkpoint, validates the chain, then atomically appends twin_lineage_promotion_v1 and switches active-lineage and all latest indexes. Superseded history remains queryable.
decisions.rationale: A promotion record gives append-only authority to the mutable active pointer, and atomic promotion prevents mixed old/new reads.
rejected_alternatives: Update old State rows or delete superseded history
Switch active-lineage index without an append-only promotion record
invariants: candidate lineage is not active until complete validation
promotion is one transaction
old lineage remains queryable
default reads use active lineage

decisions.title: Forecast aggregate and point storage
decisions.decision: twin_forecast_run_v1 is one canonical aggregate. COMPLETED requires exactly 72 ordered points with horizons 1 through 72 and excludes t0. BLOCKED or FAILED requires zero points and non-empty reason_codes and is not a successful forecast. Point rows are rebuildable projections. Forecast aggregate and projections commit atomically in State Tick Commit.
decisions.rationale: One aggregate prevents partial canonical forecasts while point projections support efficient reads; explicit failure shape avoids fabricated points.
rejected_alternatives: 72 independently committed canonical point facts
Partial successful forecast or t0 as point zero
invariants: COMPLETED means exactly 72 points
+1h through +72h only
BLOCKED or FAILED means zero points plus reason_codes
Forecast is not Scenario or Recommendation

decisions.title: Scenario aggregate and option storage
decisions.decision: twin_scenario_set_v1 is a separate atomic aggregate downstream of a committed forecast. Gate A fixes NO_ACTION, IRRIGATE_NOW_10MM, IRRIGATE_NOW_20MM, IRRIGATE_NOW_30MM, and DELAY_24H_20MM; each successful comparable option has 72 points. Scenario commit is separate from State Tick Commit and CUSTOM_OPERATOR_OPTION is deferred.
decisions.rationale: Scenario failure must not roll back State progression and Scenario must remain distinct from recommendation and action.
rejected_alternatives: Scenario inside State Tick Commit
Scenario directly creates recommendation, approval, or AO-ACT
invariants: fixed five Gate-A options
Scenario is not Recommendation
Scenario commit is downstream and separate
CUSTOM_OPERATOR_OPTION is deferred

decisions.title: Residual and calibration separation
decisions.decision: Assimilation residual belongs to the current-tick assimilation update and may update posterior uncertainty. Forecast residual belongs to twin_forecast_residual_v1 after later Evidence is matched to a historical point and never mutates that forecast. Residuals may create reviews or candidates but cannot activate models, change historical State, or prove causal effect.
decisions.rationale: The two residuals have different temporal and governance roles; conflation would corrupt history and silently change models.
rejected_alternatives: One residual object for assimilation and forecast verification
Residual directly changes active model or historical forecast
invariants: assimilation residual is not calibration
forecast residual does not mutate forecast
candidate is not active model

decisions.title: Model/config binding and activation
decisions.decision: Each tick resolves one immutable twin_runtime_config_v1 before evidence freeze and references its id/hash in outputs. Candidate, shadow evaluation, governance approval, and twin_model_activation_v1 are separate append-only transitions. Activation is effective only at a tick boundary. Rollback appends a new activation record; the mutable active index is backed by activation facts.
decisions.rationale: A tick must not mix versions, and mutable active pointers need append-only authority and later consumption proof.
rejected_alternatives: Read active parameters repeatedly during a tick
Candidate self-activation or rollback by deleting history
invariants: config is frozen before compute
activation changes only at tick boundary
later outputs prove active-config consumption
no automatic activation

decisions.title: Read API and human write API separation
decisions.decision: The frontend remains /operator/fields/:fieldId/* and the server read family is /api/v1/operator/fields/:fieldId/runtime with read-only subresources. Default lineage is active and include_superseded is false. Generated State, Forecast, Scenario, Residual, Checkpoint, and Activation objects have no public write endpoints. Human decision, approval, plan, AO-ACT, receipt, and acceptance remain separate governed writes.
decisions.rationale: Extending the existing field-scoped family avoids a parallel namespace and prevents browser submission of generated objects.
rejected_alternatives: New /operator/twin-runtime/fields frontend family
Public HTTP tick or generated-object write endpoints
invariants: Operator runtime APIs are read-only
manual tick begins as application service, CLI/manual runner, and scheduler adapter
human writes remain separately governed

decisions.title: Legacy compatibility and migration
decisions.decision: Existing evidence-twin, /api/v1/operator/twin/*, /operator/twin/*, /app/operator/*/evidence-twin, water_state_estimate_v1, and legacy scenario indexes remain compatibility reads only. No new MCFT capability or write enters them. Compatibility projections must be explicitly derived. Deletion requires caller inventory, replacement, frontend switch, runtime acceptance, completed window, and approved deletion task.
decisions.rationale: Active callers and read contracts make immediate deletion unsafe, while continued feature growth would create dual mainlines.
rejected_alternatives: Delete legacy routes in DT-02
Dual-write legacy and new canonical State or Scenario
invariants: legacy reads remain available
legacy paths gain no new capability
no dual canonical write
replacement is proven before deletion

decisions.title: Runtime security and write authority
decisions.decision: Write authority is object-specific and server-side. Evidence ingress writes Evidence; Runtime Orchestrator writes windows, transitions, posterior, forecast, tick, checkpoint, and health; Scenario Runtime writes scenario sets; Residual Matcher writes forecast residuals; Calibration writes candidates; approved Activation Service writes activation; authenticated human and governed action/executor services retain decision/action writes. Checkpoint additionally requires fencing and expected-checkpoint CAS.
decisions.rationale: Semantic boundaries are enforceable only when every object has one authorized writer class.
rejected_alternatives: Browser or sensor writes generated runtime objects
Scenario, Forecast, Receipt, or Candidate crosses its authority boundary
invariants: single writer class per object
read routes have no write authority
checkpoint writes are fenced
decision, approval, dispatch, execution, and validation remain separate

decisions.title: Runtime health and claim boundaries
decisions.decision: twin_runtime_health_v1 is append-only runtime-operation audit with a rebuildable latest index and is not physical, crop, soil, device, or model-correctness State. twin_runtime_attempt_v1 is append-only OPERATIONAL_AUDIT and never joins the posterior chain. Mutable lease, attempt-latest, and heartbeat remain coordination. Blocked, failed, and recovering attempts append attempt/health audit without advancing checkpoint.
decisions.rationale: Failures and recovery must be auditable without inventing failed State ticks or confusing runtime availability with agronomic condition.
rejected_alternatives: Failed attempt becomes canonical State tick
Health exists only as mutable state or is presented as crop/soil health
invariants: attempt is operational audit, not State history
health is runtime operation, not physical State
failed or blocked attempt creates no posterior and advances no checkpoint
stale writer cannot report committed success
